summary: Antonycc (trust: unknown) claims two separate pushes to the main branch of repository polycode-public/2-kyu-create-markdown-compiler. The first, commit f402e509 from June 16, 2026, by Antony Cartwright, updates several files and includes a message regarding showcasing the API. The second, commit b643a4fe, also from June 16, 2026, by Antony Cartwright Antonycc (trust: unknown) claims there were multiple pushes to the main branch of the polycode-public/2-kyu-create-markdown-compiler repository. Another push on 2026-06-16 involved Commit f402e509 by Antony Cartwright "showcase: src/web exercises the delivered API (distinct screenshot) + demo() + test.yml gate." Antony Cartwright pushed commits f402e509 on 2026-06-16, touching .github/workflows/test, and antonycc (trust: unknown) claims: Visitor: Issue #13 on polycode-public/2-kyu-create-markdown-compiler is closed, focusing on adding fenced code blocks and blockquotes, updating the tokenize function, and keeping existing tests green. Issue #17 is closed, addressing task lists, auto-linked URLs, a sample document in `docs/examples/sample.html`, and well-formed output checks, including disabled checkboxes for completed items and HTML entity escaping. Issue #19 is closed, adding `docs/examples/sample.md` which exercises all 10 GFM feature areas. The rendered `sample.html` is committed and verified. Issue #21 is closed, delivering the core foundation of the Markdown compiler with `tokenize`, `compile`, block headings, inline styles, and XSS-safety. Issue #23 is closed, adding links and images. Issue #25 is closed, adding lists (ordered, unordered, nested) to the Markdown compiler. Issue #27 is closed, adding code blocks and blockquotes to the Markdown compiler. Issue #31 is closed, finalizing the implementation with task lists, auto-linked URLs, and ensuring `docs/examples/sample.md` compiles to `docs/examples/sample.html`. It includes a script to regenerate the sample and a well-formedness test for balanced tags. antonycc (trust: unknown) claims: Visitor: [PR #18 merged: feat: deliver intent (deliver-intent)] and [PR #22 merged: feat: deliver intent (deliver-intent)]. PR #18 merged into main fixes issue #17. PR #22 merged into main fixes issue #21. Automated one-shot delivery via the agentic-lib transform engine. The first three involved commits by github-actions[bot] touching various files. The first commit, db21a981 by github-actions[bot] on 2026-06-17, features deliver intent and touches generate-sample.js, README.md, src/lib/main.js, and tests/unit/main. Antonycc (trust: unknown) claims: Visitor has seen Issue #5 "Deliver the INTENT" closed on repository polycode-public/2-kyu-create-markdown-compiler with ticket #5. Pull requests #8, #10, #12, #16, #18, and #22 merged agentic-lib/deliver-intent-7, agentic-lib/deliver-intent-9, agentic-lib/deliver-intent-11, agentic-lib/deliver-intent-15, agentic-lib/deliver-intent-17, and agentic-lib/deliver-intent-21 into main, fixing issues #7, #9, #11, #15, #17, and #21. Deliver INTENT.md implementation in src/lib/main.js with unit tests and a README follows acceptance criteria. Automated one-shot delivery via the agent Antonycc (trust: unknown) claims a total of nineteen separate pushes to the main branch of the repository polycode-public/2-kyu-create-markdown-compiler. The initial four commits dated 2026-06-14 include: 206444ed adds on-screenshot: publish SCREENSHOT_INDEX.png for the showcase and affects.github/workflows/on-screenshot.yml; 87a651ed changes the Dependabot update interval from weekly to monthly and modifies.github/dependabot.yml; 9a93d3ab, with the message "Refresh tooling: engine CLI scripts + marginalia-seon MCP + latest deps," touches .mcp.json, package-lock.json, and package.json; and 358c0e00 adds an on-init workflow (remote agentic-lib init) and affects.github/workflows/on-init.yml. Three new commits dated 2026-06-15 include 3812462f by intention-benchmark with the message "benchmark reset: clean baseline (2-kyu-create-markdown-compiler)," which touches AGENTS.md, agentic-lib.toml, src/lib/main.js, src/web/index.html, src/web/lib.js, and two more files. Additionally, there are ten commits by github-actions[bot]: 019dc2ba with the message "feat: deliver intent (deliver-intent) (#8)" touching README.md, src/lib/main.js, and tests/unit/main.test; e79e6058 with the message "feat: deliver intent (deliver-intent) (#14)" touching README.md; Antonycc reported issue #7 concerning core features and XSS-safety, which was closed. Issue #9, related to links and images, was also closed after adding inline links and images with HTML-escaping. Issue #11, concerning lists, was opened and subsequently closed, ensuring proper rendering of unordered, ordered, and nested lists. Issue #15, focusing on chunk 5/6 of the Markdown compiler, has been opened and closed, adding tables with alignment and horizontal rules to the Markdown compiler. This slice added GFM tables with headers, delimiters, and alignment, as well as horizontal rules rendered as `<hr/>`. The `tokenize` function was updated accordingly. Acceptance criteria involved rendering tests for tables and horizontal rules, ensuring inline formatting within table cells, maintaining existing tests, and updating the README. Antonycc reported that issue #15 has been closed, with chunk 5 of 6 complete, focusing on tables (alignment) and horizontal rules. This slice was delivered, keeping existing tests green. Now, issue #29 has been opened and closed for chunk 5/6, adding tables with alignment and horizontal rules to the Markdown compiler. The scope includes GFM pipe tables with headers, delimiter rows, and alignment, as well as horizontal rules rendered as `<hr/>`. The `tokenize` function was updated to accommodate these changes. Acceptance criteria require a 2-col table with `|:--|--:|` to render `<table>` with left/right aligned cells, `compile("---")` to return `<hr/>`, and ensure inline formatting in cells works, keeping existing tests green and updating the README. Antonycc (trust: unknown) claims: Visitor has opened two issues on the repository polycode-public/2-kyu-create-markdown-compiler. Issue #9 involves adding links and images to the Markdown compiler, focusing on converting inline links and images while ensuring existing tests remain green. Issue #11, now closed in chunk 3 of 6, focuses on adding lists, including unordered, ordered, and nested lists, ensuring inline formatting and links work within list items and existing tests stay green. The scope includes support for unordered lists with `-`, `*`, `+`, ordered lists with `1.`, `2.`, and nested lists through indentation.
